title: sanguisorba muricata subsp. ertugrulii
intro: Sanguisorba muricata subsp. ertugrulii, also known as the Ertugrul's burnet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Turkey. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat external wounds and injuries due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects. The plant is known for its health benefits, including the promotion of wound healing, reduction of inflammation, and prevention of infection. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Sanguisorba muricata subsp. ertugrulii are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to aid in the recovery of burns, cuts, and other skin irritations.
